data.text/html:
COOTEFOO Bias Analysis

Visual analytics dashboard · Haacklee Herald · FILAH data TROUT data Full journalist data

Part 1 — Are the accusations supported by each group's own data?

Each dataset records discussion participation sentiment tagged by industry (tourism / large vessel / small vessel). A member with consistently positive sentiment toward tourism and negative toward fishing would confirm FILAH's bias claim; the reverse would confirm TROUT's. The heatmap and bar charts below surface those patterns.

Part 1 — Verdict: Are accusations supported?
⚠ FILAH's accusation — pro-tourism bias
FILAH claims certain members favour tourism. Their own data shows:
Carol Limpet: tourism avg=+0.70, fishing avg=+0.75 → neutral
Seal: tourism avg=+0.10, fishing avg=+0.05 → neutral
Simone Kat: tourism avg=+0.88, fishing avg=-0.15 → pro-tourism
FILAH's dataset records only trips to commercial/tourism zones for the three members it tracks (Simone Kat, Carol Limpet, Seal), inflating the appearance of tourism-focused travel.
⚠ TROUT's accusation — pro-fishing bias
TROUT claims certain members favour fishing industry. Their own data shows:
Ed Helpsford: tourism avg=+0.71, fishing avg=+1.00 → pro-fishing
Seal: tourism avg=+0.10, fishing avg=+0.05 → neutral
Teddy Goldstein: tourism avg=-0.50, fishing avg=+0.93 → pro-fishing
TROUT's dataset captures all government-zone trips (official meetings) but records almost no commercial-zone trips, making members appear purely administrative rather than industry-aligned.

Part 3 — Accusations in context of the full journalist dataset

The journalist's graph adds 4 meetings (13-16), extra trips, and a 6th member (Tante Titan) invisible to both FILAH and TROUT. The charts below let you compare each member's profile across all three data sources simultaneously.
Per-member sentiment — all three datasets side-by-side
Per-member travel zones — all three datasets side-by-side
